title: Settings
subtitle: Manage your profile, organization, and team access.

profile.section_subtitle: Details visible to the rest of your team.

organization.section_subtitle: The workspace that owns your projects and catalogues.
organization.name: Name
organization.no_org_title: You're not in an organization yet
organization.no_org_hint: Create one to start building projects and catalogues.

members.section_subtitle: Invite teammates, edit their access, and revoke it when needed.
members.list_title: {count, plural, =0 {No members yet} one {# member} other {# members}}
members.invitations_hint: {count, plural, =0 {No invitations out right now} one {# invite awaiting acceptance} other {# invites awaiting acceptance}}.
members.remove_body: {name} will lose access to this organization immediately. Their work stays intact — you can re-invite them later.
members.access_denied_body: Ask an admin to grant you the members.view capability to see the team roster.

modules.members_description: Who else can sign in, invite new people, and change these permissions.
modules.catalogues_description: Raw materials, packaging, and any custom reference tables — scoped per catalogue.
modules.formulations_description: Build projects, save versions, approve spec sheets, run QC validations.
modules.proposals_description: Customer-facing proposal pipeline — list, edit, approve, sign, browse approvals queue and signed archive. Split from Projects so commercial roles can own this workflow without project-edit rights.
modules.audit_description: Read the org-wide trail of every write across catalogues, projects, spec sheets, trial batches, and QC validations.
modules.no_row_scopes: No catalogues exist yet — create one to scope access.

members.view: See the team roster and each member's role.
members.invite: Send invitations to new teammates by email.
members.edit_permissions: Change which capabilities other members have.
members.remove: Revoke a member's access to this organization.

catalogues.view: Open the catalogue table and read its rows.
catalogues.edit: Create, edit, and archive individual rows.
catalogues.import: Run the bulk Excel import to load many rows at once.
catalogues.manage_fields: Add, edit, or archive attribute definitions (the columns of the catalogue).
catalogues.delete: Permanently remove rows. Cannot be undone.

formulations.view: Read formulations, spec sheets, proposals, trial batches, and QC records.
formulations.edit: Create and edit formulations, spec sheets, proposals, trial batches, and QC records.
formulations.approve: Move documents through draft → in review → approved → sent. Required to send a proposal or spec sheet to a customer.
formulations.delete: Permanently remove a formulation or any of its child documents.
formulations.view_approvals: Open the org-wide inbox showing every document currently waiting for a signature.
formulations.view_signed: Open the customer-signed archive showing what's been sent out and what's come back signed.
formulations.assign_sales_person: Pick or change the commercial owner (sales rep) of a project. Split out so sales managers can reassign accounts without spec-edit rights.
formulations.manage_spec_visibility: Toggle individual sections (Nutrition, Microbiology, etc.) on or off for the customer-facing spec sheet.
formulations.sign_spec: Apply your signature to a spec sheet or proposal in any internal slot (Scientist, R&D Manager, Product Manager, Director). Without this, the signature pad is read-only.
formulations.comments_view: Read comment threads on any project entity.
formulations.comments_write: Post comments and reply. You can always edit, delete, or resolve your own threads.
formulations.comments_moderate: Edit, delete, or resolve OTHER users' comment threads. The moderator role — typically team leads only.

proposals.view: Read the proposals list, individual proposals, and the customer-facing PDF.
proposals.edit: Create proposals, edit lines, customer info, pricing, and acknowledgements. Render and re-render the PDF.
proposals.approve: Move a proposal through draft → in review → approved → sent. Required to send the customer signing link.
proposals.sign: Apply your signature to a proposal in any internal slot (Scientist, R&D Manager, Product Manager, Director). The customer signature on the public kiosk is gated by the link, not by this capability.
proposals.view_approvals: Open the proposals tab of the org-wide approvals inbox showing every proposal waiting for a signature.
proposals.view_signed: Open the proposals tab of the customer-signed archive showing what's been sent out and what's come back signed.
proposals.assign_sales_person: Pick or change the commercial owner (sales rep) on a proposal's parent project. Split out so sales managers can reassign accounts without proposal-edit rights.

audit.view: Read the org-wide log of every write across catalogues, projects, spec sheets, trial batches, and QC.

role_presets.members: Owners have all of these implicitly. Most members only need View. Grant Invite and Edit permissions to admins / team leads.
role_presets.catalogues: Scientist: View + Edit on raw_materials & packaging. Bulk import for whoever loads new supplier sheets. Manage fields and Hard delete are admin-only.
role_presets.formulations: Scientist: View, Edit, Sign spec sheet, Comments view + write. R&D Manager / Director: everything above + Approve & send + Comments moderate. Read-only stakeholder: View only. (Sales typically does not need any Projects capability — they live in the Proposals module instead.)
role_presets.proposals: Sales: View, Edit, View approvals queue, View signed, Assign sales person. Sales lead: everything above + Approve & send. Director: all of the above + Sign proposal. Scientist: usually only Sign proposal so they can sign the internal slot when handed a draft. Read-only stakeholder: View only.
role_presets.audit: Compliance and QA leads. Owners get it implicitly.
